headline: Fund manager bought Nvidia, SK Hynix; sold software - 'Follow the money' into AI hardware
detail: A credible real-money manager is publicly rotating from software into AI hardware names, validating the thesis that chip/infrastructure is where AI spending lands first. This directly supports your SMH and SOXL holdings in theory, but the fact that your chip ETFs are still negative while this narrative runs hot is a warning - you're not getting credit for it yet. If SMH can't rally on this news, that's a red flag for the position.
rec: No action yet - watch SMH today. If it catches a bid and SOXL bounces above $217, you could tighten stops and let it ride. Scanner shows TSM ACCUMULATE 7/12 (entry $443.34-$443.79, stop $442.47) as a pure-play hardware name if you want direct exposure, but only if SMH confirms the move first.

headline: ARM scanner setup - ACCUMULATE 7/12, strong AI infrastructure link
detail: ARM designs the chip architecture powering AI inference across mobile and data center; it sits at the center of the 'follow the money' AI hardware theme in today's headlines. The scanner flags a grade B daily/4H uptrend with an order block at $303-$337 and entry near $336.80-$337.13. As a new buy it would add more chip concentration to an already 90% tech book on margin.
rec: If you want a direct AI hardware play with a scanner setup, ARM entry $336.80-$337.13, stop $336.18 is the cleanest name tied to today's catalyst - but size tiny given you're already heavy chips on margin. Idea to consider, not advice.

headline: Google loses $4.7B EU antitrust fine appeal
detail: Big Tech regulatory risk is back in the headlines - this keeps pressure on mega-cap platform names and reminds the market that EU antitrust is a real, ongoing cost for large tech. Your QQQ and SPY holdings have broad mega-cap exposure; this is unlikely to move them materially today but adds to the regulatory overhang narrative. Not a chip-specific event, but sentiment drag on broad tech is worth noting.
rec: No action - this doesn't directly hit your chip or pure-AI names, and QQQ/SPY stops ($670/$705) are far away. Just background noise for your book today.

headline: SpaceX blueprint for mega-cap IPOs - new wave coming
detail: If SpaceX-style mega IPOs become a trend, it could pull capital away from existing tech/chip holdings as investors rebalance into new names. More immediately, it reinforces that private AI infrastructure companies are building massive scale - positive for chip demand long-term. No direct near-term trade for your current book.
rec: No action now - this is a longer-term theme. Watch for any IPO announcements that could suck liquidity from your names, but nothing to trade today.
